body.purpose: Default UI copy, table cells, KPI values.

label.purpose: Column headers, form labels, status badges.

shadows.name: resting
shadows.purpose: Default for cards and table containers, the vast majority of the UI.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Above-flow but non-modal elements: map's Route Filter panel, View Algorithm button.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: True modals and popups: Fleet Config, routing params, Algorithm Comparison popup.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default transition easing for hover/color state changes across buttons and nav items.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main calculation/confirm CTA, e.g. คำนวณเส้นทาง.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary">คำนวณเส้นทาง</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1E3A8A; color: #ffffff; padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 8px; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.875rem; border: none; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1e40af; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1E3A8A; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Success Button
components.description: Explicit save/apply action, e.g. Fleet Config's save button.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-success">💾 บันทึกและใช้งานกองรถนี้</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-success { background: #10B981; color: #ffffff; padding: 10px 24px; border-radius: 8px; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.875rem; border: none; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-success:hover { background: #059669; } .ds-btn-success:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #10B981; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-ghost">🔄 ใช้ค่ามาตรฐาน (Standard)</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: #e2e8f0; color: #334155; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 6px; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.875rem; border: none; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #cbd5e1; }

components.name: Comparison Table Cell (best value)
components.description: The single strongest visual signal in the app: green tint marking the winning value in a comparison column.
components.css: .ds-cell-best { background: #d1fae5; color: #065f46; font-weight: 700; padding: 12px 16px; text-align: right; border-radius: 4px; }

components.name: Card
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.05); padding: 24px; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: #64748b; margin: 0 0 8px 0; } .ds-card-value { font-size: 1.875rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1E3A8A; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Standard text/number field, e.g. Fleet Config's capacity/fuel inputs.
components.css: .ds-input { border: 1px solid #cbd5e1; border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 16px; font-size: 0.875rem; background: #ffffff; color: #1e293b; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #1E3A8A; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(30,58,138,0.3); }

components.name: Nav Item (active)
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; width: 100%; padding: 10px 16px; border-radius: 8px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; border: none; background: transparent; color: #334155; transition: background 0.2s; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #f1f5f9; } .ds-nav-item--active { background: #1E3A8A; color: #ffffff; }

components.name: Overlay Modal Shell
components.description: Backdrop + panel shell shared by Fleet Config, routing params, and Algorithm Comparison popup.
components.css: .ds-modal-backdrop { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(15,23,42,0.5); backdrop-filter: blur(4px); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-modal-panel { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 16px; box-shadow: 0 20px 25px rgba(0,0,0,0.15); padding: 24px; max-width: 32rem; width: 100%; } .ds-modal-title { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1E3A8A; margin: 0; }

narrative.overview: RouteWay Intelligence reads like the console a dispatcher trusts to plan a real fleet's day: dense with numbers that must be scannable and citable, never decorative for its own sake. The system is built around one dominant fleet navy that carries structure and authority, a single signal green reserved for 'this is the best number on the row' and confirmed actions, and a flat, bordered surface language that keeps 8-9 rows of algorithm comparison legible at a glance rather than fighting the eye with gradients or glass. This explicitly rejects generic SaaS-dashboard tropes: no gradient text, no hero-metric-with-gradient-accent cards, no identical icon-heading-text card grids repeated for their own sake, no glassmorphism as decoration.
keyCharacteristics: One dominant brand color (Fleet Navy) carrying headings, primary actions, and active nav state
Signal green is earned, not default — it marks the winning value in a comparison, not 'primary' branding
Flat cards with hairline borders and shadow-sm at rest; elevation increases only for things that float above the page
Data density over whitespace-for-its-own-sake — this is a working tool, not a marketing surface

rules.name: The Earned Green Rule
rules.body: Signal green is never a default accent color. It appears only where it marks a specific winning value, a successful save, or an on-time status.

rules.name: The Categorical-Never-Semantic Rule
rules.body: Route Blue and Route Orange identify a truck's route on the map and must never be reinterpreted as info/warning colors elsewhere.

rules.name: The Scan-First Rule
rules.body: Every number that feeds a business decision must be at Body weight or heavier and never below 0.875rem.

rules.name: The Elevation-Equals-Z-Index Rule
rules.body: A component's shadow weight must match its z-index tier: resting for in-flow content, floating for above-flow controls, overlay for true modals/popups.

dos: Do reserve Signal Green exclusively for winning value, on-time, or confirmed save
Do match shadow tier to z-index tier
Do keep every cost/CO2/distance number at Body weight or larger
Do use borders, not shadows, to separate adjacent cards in dense layouts
Do use rounded-lg (8px) as the default corner radius
donts: Don't use gradient text or gradient-accented hero metric cards
Don't build identical icon+heading+text card grids as a default layout pattern
Don't reuse Route Blue or Route Orange as semantic info/warning colors
Don't reach for a modal as the first instinct for new features
Don't encode delivery status with color alone
